title: Global Sports Report
headline: MLB Live Board Anchors Busy Night Across Baseball, NBA and Global Soccer
snapshot: Major League Baseball leads an active sports board featuring live leverage situations, late-night pitching matchups, NBA positioning and a heavy global soccer slate.
key_storylines: MLB slate features 10 live games and 5 upcoming matchups
NBA board mixes live action with a late Rockets-Lakers matchup
NHL board includes 2 live games and 2 upcoming games
Global soccer remains highly active with 56 finals, 2 live matches and 10 upcoming fixtures
Advanced pitching, efficiency and EPA signals continue shaping newsroom angles

live_newsroom.label: MLB
live_newsroom.headline: Late MLB Pitching Matchups Drive Newsroom Focus
live_newsroom.summary: The MLB board is active with live game movement, developing leverage spots and a late schedule built around confirmed probable starters.
key_data: MLB board shows 15 total games in this report window
10 games were live during the report window
5 upcoming games listed probable starters on both sides
Dodgers vs Giants features Yoshinobu Yamamoto vs Landen Roupp
Padres vs Rockies features Randy Vásquez vs Jimmy Herget
why_it_matters: Confirmed starters give reporters stronger footing for preview coverage
Live action shifts the editorial lens toward leverage moments, bullpen usage and in-game momentum
The late window keeps MLB coverage active deep into the night
what_to_watch: Monitor bullpen leverage and late-inning momentum swings
Track probable-starter confirmations before first pitch
Watch Statcast pitching signals, whiff rate and spin-rate context

live_newsroom.label: NFL
live_newsroom.headline: NFL Advanced Layer Keeps Team-Building Signals in Focus
live_newsroom.summary: With no live NFL games available, the strongest football angle comes from EPA, pass efficiency, rush efficiency and draft-position leverage.
key_data: New England led the available EPA/play board at 0.149
New England also posted the strongest pass-efficiency signal at 0.324 pass EPA per play
Baltimore showed one of the strongest available rushing-efficiency signals
Draft structure remains a key team-building frame in non-game windows
why_it_matters: EPA/play gives a stronger snapshot of down-to-down value than raw yardage
Pass and rush efficiency help separate true team strengths from surface-level narratives
Draft leverage matters when teams are shaping roster construction
what_to_watch: Monitor quarterback and offensive-line storylines near the draft
Track how prior-season EPA profiles shape early matchup expectations
Watch roster construction signals tied to draft capital
